title: Gate Manager Output Schema
description: Structured output produced by the Gate Manager after evaluating a proposed execution plan against all governance gates.

gate_results.description: Individual evaluation result for each governance gate that was assessed.

gate_name.description: The name of the governance gate (e.g., 'scope-check', 'security-check', 'cost-check').

status.description: The outcome of this gate evaluation. 'pass' means the plan satisfies this gate. 'needs-review' means a human must assess before proceeding. 'blocked' means execution cannot proceed until this gate condition is resolved.

reason.description: Explanation of why this gate produced its status. Should reference specific policy rules, thresholds, or observed conditions.

evidence.description: Specific evidence items that support the gate decision. Each item is a concrete observation, measurement, or policy reference.

remediation.description: Optional: what must happen to resolve a needs-review or blocked status.

overall_status.description: Aggregate status across all gate evaluations. 'blocked' if any gate is blocked. 'needs-review' if no gates are blocked but at least one needs review. 'pass' only if all gates pass.

blocking_gates.description: Names of all gates whose status is 'blocked'. Execution must not proceed until all blocking gates are resolved.

review_required_gates.description: Names of all gates whose status is 'needs-review'. These require explicit human or policy approval before execution continues.

recommendation.description: The Gate Manager's recommendation for how to proceed given the gate evaluation results.

action.description: The recommended action. 'proceed' if all gates pass. 'escalate-for-review' if any gate needs review. 'halt' if any gate is blocked.

rationale.description: Explanation of the recommendation, including what must happen before execution can proceed if the action is not 'proceed'.

escalation_target.description: Optional: the role or system to which this should be escalated if action is 'escalate-for-review'.
